catalog.title: Listening · From gist to proof in one clip
catalog.description: From gist to proof in one clip · A2 listening in “Health & body”: Dutch you can use the same week, guided steps, and a short check at the end.

pedagogy.objective: By the end of this lesson, you can follow a longer listening clip about health & body, catching details, numbers, and speaker intentions
can_do_outcomes: Ask for over-the-counter medicine with basic constraints
React appropriately to advice (rust, water drinken, paracetamol)
Track details in a longer clip using the transcript.

examples_nl: Je wast je handen voor het eten.
Ik voel me vandaag niet zo goed.
examples_en: You wash your hands before eating.
I don't feel so well today.

grammar_points.point: modal zullen for soft advice (Je zult moeten …) — recognition
examples_nl: Ik ben naar de huisartsenpraktijk gebeld.
Gisteren had ik koorts en een zere keel.
examples_en: Yesterday I had a fever and a sore throat.

grammar_points.point: past tense recognition (ik had pijn) for narratives
examples_nl: Ik heb vandaag al drie liter water gedronken.
De assistent vraagt of ik paracetamol verdraag.
examples_en: I've already drunk three litres of water today.
The assistant asks if I tolerate paracetamol.

micro_outcomes: I can recognise key words about health & body in short Dutch texts or audio.
I can copy at least one useful chunk into a sentence about myself.
I can finish most guided tasks in the lesson without peeking at translations on the second try.
I can pass the quick self-check at the end of the lesson.
I know what to revise tomorrow in two minutes or less.

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Follow a longer listening clip about health & body, catching details, numbers, and speaker intentions

**Grammar thread today:** Perfectum (hebben/zijn + voltooid deelwoord) — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Health & body**.

Words you might use today:
• **paracetamol**
• **rust**
• **afspraak**
• **huisarts**
• **spoed**
• **koorts**
• **hoofdpijn**
• **keel**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.example_response: Gisteren voelde ik me niet zo goed en bleef ik thuis. Ik dronk veel thee en rustte 's middags even. 's Avonds ging het al wat beter.

steps.activity:
**Listening focus**

Play the audio twice if you can (slow speed first is fine).

**Transcript** (read along on the second listen):

Assistent: Huisartsenpraktijk Noord, goedemorgen.
Patiënt: Goedemorgen, ik ben verkouden en mijn keel doet pijn sinds maandag.
Assistent: Heeft u koorts gemeten?
Patiënt: Gisteren 37,8. Ik hoest ook een beetje.
Assistent: Dokter kan u om 15:20 zien. Neem uw zorgpas mee.
Patiënt: Prima, dank u wel.

**While you listen:** underline in your mind who wants what and any numbers you hear.

---

**Script — huisarts phone (fragments)**

*Assistent:* Huisartsenpraktijk De Linde, goedemiddag.
*Patiënt:* Goedemiddag, ik wil graag een afspraak. Ik ben al drie dagen verkouden.

**Gloss:** *praktijk* = practice · *verkouden* = having a cold.
**Listen task:** note one symptom and the politeness chunk you hear.

illustration.alt: Headphones icon suggesting focused listening before reading the transcript

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — perfectum (short)**

**Rule:** *hebben/zijn + voltooid deelwoord* for completed experiences: *Ik **heb** gewerkt*, *Ik **ben** gekomen*.

**Patterns:**
• *Gisteren heb ik boodschappen gedaan.* · *Ben je op tijd gekomen?*

**Produce:**
1. One sentence: what you did yesterday (*gisteren heb ik …*).
2. One question with *heb je …* or *ben je …*.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — g / ch:** practise *goed — licht — dag — nacht* with TTS; back *g* vs softer *ch*.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**reflexive verbs (zich voelen, zich wassen)**
• NL: Je wast je handen voor het eten. / Ik voel me vandaag niet zo goed.
• EN: You wash your hands before eating. / I don't feel so well today.

**modal zullen for soft advice (Je zult moeten …) — recognition**
• NL: Ik ben naar de huisartsenpraktijk gebeld. / Gisteren had ik koorts en een zere keel.
• EN: I called the GP practice. / Yesterday I had a fever and a sore throat.

**past tense recognition (ik had pijn) for narratives**
• NL: Ik heb vandaag al drie liter water gedronken. / De assistent vraagt of ik paracetamol verdraag.
• EN: I've already drunk three litres of water today. / The assistant asks if I tolerate paracetamol.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.

**1 — Before listening:** guess three words you might hear (write them).

**2 — Gist:** is this about work, family, travel, or shopping?

**3 — Numbers:** write every number or time you hear in the script.

**4 — Who:** who takes which action (choose: A / B / both)?

**5 — Polite or informal:** find one **u**-sentence and one **je/jij**-sentence.

**6 — Paraphrase:** in English, one sentence — what do they want?

**7 — Shadowing:** read one speaker's lines aloud with the transcript.

**8 — New chunk:** pick one phrase to use in your own week.

**Script reminder:**
Assistent: Huisartsenpraktijk Noord, goedemorgen.
Patiënt: Goedemorgen, ik ben verkouden en mijn keel doet pijn sinds maandag.
Assistent: Heeft u koorts gemeten?
Patiënt: Gisteren 37,8. Ik hoest ook een beetje.
Assistent: Dokter kan u om 15:20 zien. Neem uw zorgpas mee.
Patiënt: Prima, dank u wel.
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Assistent: Huisartsenpraktijk Noord, goedemorgen.*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Assistent: Huisartsenpraktijk Noord, goedemorgen.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **paracetamol** and fits *Perfectum (hebben/zijn + voltooid deelwoord)*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

steps.activity:
**Phrase bank (use freely)**
• paracetamol
• rust
• afspraak
• huisarts
• spoed
• koorts
• hoofdpijn
• keel
• neus
• verkouden

**Your task:** Write or say **4–6 short sentences** in Dutch about this unit theme.
Rules: include **at least one question** and one connector (**en**, **maar**, or **want**).

**Example starter (you can change it):**
Vandaag … Ik wil … **want** … **En jij?**

**Quick self-check**

Try each question, then tap **Check answer**. The correct answer stays hidden until you submit.

**Tip:** Say Dutch options out loud if that helps.

**Afterwards:** If three or more felt easy, open **Quiz** for a short recap. If not, go back to **Guided practice** once more.
steps.teacher_notes: Rendered with interactive UI when interaction is present.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **paracetamol**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag paracetamol met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next in this unit: **Writing · Emails & apps: polite & clear** — we keep building **Health & body** with a new focus.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

Sport and **bewegen** are mainstream small talk. *Ik ga naar de fysio* or *Ik moet meer stappen* are easy ways to bond lightly.

**Phrases — GP & pharmacy**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Ik wil graag een afspraak maken.* | I'd like to make an appointment. |
| *Ik ben verkouden sinds maandag.* | I've had a cold since Monday. |
| *Hoe vaak moet ik deze pillen innemen?* | How often should I take these pills? |

**Mini dialogue**

**Assistent:** Huisartsenpraktijk Noord, goedemiddag.
**Patiënt:** Goedemiddag, ik heb last van mijn keel en lichte koorts.
**Assistent:** Dokter kan u om 16:10 zien. Neem uw zorgpas mee.

**Say or write:** Name one symptom + duration in Dutch.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?
steps.teacher_notes: Link to next lesson id in CMS when wired.

quiz_ideas: Self-check: choose the best reply to "Hoe gaat het?" (see step 7 in the lesson).
Gap-fill idea: write a sentence with **paracetamol** in the present tense.
True/false: in Dutch yes/no questions the verb often comes before the subject.
Say one new polite request starting with **Kunt u …** or **Mag ik …**.
Paraphrase one Dutch line from the lesson input in your own English.
assessment.success_criteria: You can complete the step-7 self-check comfortably and produce at least one clear Dutch sentence about the lesson theme (spoken or written).

content_refs.notes: Slow audio assumed for lines marked *slow* in builder CMS.

Patiënt: Goedemorgen, ik ben verkouden en mijn keel doet pijn sinds maandag.
Assistent: Heeft u koorts gemeten?
Patiënt: Gisteren 37,8. Ik hoest ook een beetje.
Assistent: Dokter kan u om 15:20 zien. Neem uw zorgpas mee.
Patiënt: Prima, dank u wel.
